channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How the Iran war is wasting American resources
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: How the U.S. oil blockade is taking a high toll on everyday Cubans
item.description: As tensions between Washington and Havana mount, what is life like for Cubans living through a weekslong oil blockade? NPR's Adrian Ma speaks to CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about life on the island.
item.content:encoded: As tensions between Washington and Havana mount, what is life like for Cubans living through a weekslong oil blockade? NPR's Adrian Ma speaks to CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about life on the island.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Iraqi Kurds mark Nowruz, celebrating light over darkness
item.description: In the Kurdish regions of the Middle East, Nowruz celebrations — honoring the arrival of spring — are a fundamental expression of Kurdish identity.
item.content:encoded:
In the Kurdish regions of the Middle East, Nowruz celebrations — honoring the arrival of spring — are a fundamental expression of Kurdish identity.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: End of an heir-a: The U.K. abolishes aristocrats' right to inherit Parliament seats
item.description: The British Parliament still has 92 unelected lawmakers who inherit seats by bloodline. They're all older white men. A new law now phases them out, for the first time in nearly 1,000 years.
item.content:encoded:
The British Parliament still has 92 unelected lawmakers who inherit seats by bloodline. They're all older white men. A new law now phases them out, for the first time in nearly 1,000 years.

(Image credit: Susannah Ireland for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Meet the Dutch art detective who tracks down stolen masterpieces
item.description: For 20 years, Dutch art detective Arthur Brand has acted as an intermediary between the police and people who know where stolen artwork might be hiding. He says patience and trust are everything.
item.content:encoded: For 20 years, Dutch art detective Arthur Brand has acted as an intermediary between the police and people who know where stolen artwork might be hiding. He says patience and trust are everything.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Rosman

item.title: Iran war enters its fourth week with no clear end in sight
item.description: As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering "winding down" military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.
item.content:encoded:
As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering "winding down" military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.

(Image credit: Amir Levy)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Iran's cultural heritage sites are being damaged by American and Israeli strikes
item.description: The costs of the joint U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to some of Iran's most famous and beloved cultural heritage sites.
item.content:encoded:
The costs of the joint U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran are mounting in terms of civilian deaths and damage to some of Iran's most famous and beloved cultural heritage sites.

(Image credit: Morteza Nikoubazi)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: 'Everybody was wearing black.' How the Iranian diaspora is observing Nowruz amid war
item.description: Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.
item.content:encoded:
haft sin — a traditional table set for Nowruz featuring symbolic objects.'/>

Nowruz celebrates the arrival of spring and rebirth. But for many in the Iranian diaspora, this year is different. As the war continues, many are trying to balance the joy of the holiday with grief.

(Image credit: Sarah Ventre)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Ventre

item.title: How the Iran war threatens global food supply
item.description: About a third of all fertilizer shipped globally passes through the Strait of Hormuz. Now shipping is all-but stopped through the Strait and this could have repercussions for the global food supply.
item.content:encoded:
About a third of all fertilizer shipped globally passes through the Strait of Hormuz. Now shipping is all-but stopped through the Strait and this could have repercussions for the global food supply.

(Image credit: Narinder Nanu/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Palestinians celebrate Eid in Gaza, making the most of a fragile ceasefire
item.description: The ceasefire, in effect for the past six months, has brought some reprieve to Palestinians in Gaza despite continued hardship, displacement and Israeli restrictions on aid.
item.content:encoded:
The ceasefire, in effect for the past six months, has brought some reprieve to Palestinians in Gaza despite continued hardship, displacement and Israeli restrictions on aid.

(Image credit: Anas Baba)
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba
